description: Complete redesign of the Chrome extension side panel using the exact RetroUI NeoBrutalism theme and actual RetroUI component source files from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/retroui/. Components are copied verbatim from RetroUI source with only import path changes. Uses RetroUI Empty for empty states, Alert for warnings/errors, Loader for loading, plus Button, Badge, Card, Switch, Accordion, Tooltip, Input, Text. Layout includes plugin SVG icons and per-tool lucide icons (both placeholder for now).

userStories.title: Add RetroUI dependencies and cn() utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the exact RetroUI dependency stack installed so that RetroUI components work in the side panel build
acceptanceCriteria: class-variance-authority ^0.7.0 is added to platform/browser-extension/package.json dependencies
src/side-panel/lib/utils.ts exists with the EXACT cn() utility copied from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/lib/utils.ts
bun install completes without errors from platform/browser-extension/
The existing side panel builds successfully (bun run build from root)
userStories.notes: Install from platform/browser-extension/: bun add class-variance-authority@^0.7.0 @radix-ui/react-switch@^1.1.3 @radix-ui/react-accordion@^1.2.1 @radix-ui/react-tooltip@^1.2.3 @radix-ui/react-slot@^1.2.2 clsx@^2.1.1 tailwind-merge@^2.5.3 tw-animate-css@^1.3.7. The cn() MUST be copied from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/lib/utils.ts: import clsx from 'clsx'; import { ClassNameValue, twMerge } from 'tailwind-merge'; export function cn(...classes: ClassNameValue[]) { return twMerge(clsx(classes)); }. Note: clsx uses default import, ClassNameValue from tailwind-merge.

userStories.title: Update styles.css with exact RetroUI theme (Tailwind CSS v4)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the side panel CSS to use the exact RetroUI NeoBrutalism theme from the RetroUI source code
acceptanceCriteria: styles.css contains the EXACT @theme block from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/content/docs/install/vite.mdx step 7.2 with literal font names: --font-head: 'Archivo Black', sans-serif; --font-sans: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif;

:root block has EXACT values from vite.mdx: --radius: 0, --background: #fff, --foreground: #000, --card: #fff, --card-foreground: #000, --primary: #ffdb33, --primary-hover: #ffcc00, --primary-foreground: #000, --secondary: #000, --secondary-foreground: #fff, --muted: #aeaeae, --muted-foreground: #5a5a5a, --accent: #fae583, --accent-foreground: #000, --destructive: #e63946, --destructive-foreground: #fff, --border: #000

.dark block has EXACT values from vite.mdx: --radius: 0, --background: #1a1a1a, --foreground: #f5f5f5, --card: #242424, --card-foreground: #f5f5f5, --primary: #ffdb33, --primary-hover: #ffcc00, --primary-foreground: #000, --secondary: #3a3a3a, --secondary-foreground: #f5f5f5, --muted: #3f3f46, --muted-foreground: #a0a0a0, --accent: #fae583, --accent-foreground: #000, --destructive: #e63946, --destructive-foreground: #fff, --border: #3a3a3a
Google Fonts loaded via <link> tags in side-panel.html <head>
body uses background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); font-family: var(--font-sans);
userStories.notes: CRITICAL: Copy @theme, :root, .dark EXACTLY from ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/content/docs/install/vite.mdx (step 7.2 'Add Theme', lines 141-211). Use literal font names in @theme (not var() refs). Add to side-panel.html <head>: <link rel='preconnect' href='https://fonts.googleapis.com' /><link rel='preconnect' href='https://fonts.gstatic.com' crossorigin /><link href='https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Archivo+Black&family=Space+Grotesk:wght@300..700&display=swap' rel='stylesheet' /> (from vite.mdx lines 120-128). Verify Tailwind v4 processing works in Bun.build pipeline.

userStories.title: Copy RetroUI component source files into the side panel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the exact RetroUI component source files copied and adapted so that the side panel uses real RetroUI components
acceptanceCriteria: All 'use client' directives removed (Switch, Accordion, Tooltip have them)
All @/lib/utils imports changed to relative paths
All @/components/retroui/* imports changed to relative paths within retro/ directory
CVA variants, Tailwind classes, Radix usage, component structure IDENTICAL to source
All files pass TypeScript and ESLint
userStories.notes:
11 components total. ALLOWED changes: (1) Remove 'use client'. (2) Change @/lib/utils to relative path. (3) Change @/components/retroui/* to relative ./paths. (4) Add .js extensions if needed. FORBIDDEN: Any change to class names, CVA variants, Radix props, or logic. Key source facts:
- Button.tsx: default variant uses 'border-black' (NOT border-border). Uses forwardRef + Slot.
- Input.tsx: Does NOT use cn(). Uses template literal. Classes: 'px-4 py-2 w-full rounded border-2 shadow-md transition focus:outline-hidden focus:shadow-xs'. Has aria-invalid conditional.
- Accordion.tsx: AccordionContent has hardcoded 'bg-white text-gray-700' -- does NOT adapt to dark mode. This matches source.
- Tooltip.tsx: Uses animate-in, fade-in-0, zoom-in-95 from tw-animate-css.
- Empty.tsx: Compound component (Empty, Empty.Content, Empty.Icon, Empty.Title, Empty.Separator, Empty.Description). Uses Ghost from lucide-react as default icon. Imports Text. Classes: 'flex flex-col items-center justify-center p-4 md:p-8 border-2 rounded shadow-md transition-all hover:shadow-none bg-card text-center'.
- Alert.tsx: Has variant (default/solid) and status (error/success/warning/info). Classes include status-specific colors: error='bg-red-300 text-red-800 border-red-800', warning='bg-yellow-300 text-yellow-800 border-yellow-800'. Imports Text.
- Loader.tsx: Bouncing dots with border-2 animate-bounce. Has variant (default/secondary/outline) and size (sm/md/lg). Default shows primary-colored dots.

userStories.title: Create PluginIcon placeholder component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a placeholder SVG icon for plugins so the layout reserves space for future custom plugin icons
acceptanceCriteria: Renders a 32x32 container with border-2 border-border rounded bg-muted containing a placeholder SVG (puzzle piece or similar)
Accepts pluginName: string (for future icon mapping), optional size (default 32), optional className
The SVG uses currentColor for strokes, looks intentional as a placeholder
userStories.notes: Simple component. Container: 'flex items-center justify-center border-2 border-border bg-muted shrink-0' with width/height from size prop. Inner SVG: a puzzle piece shape using stroke='currentColor'. pluginName is unused now but enables future icon lookup.

userStories.title: Create ToolIcon placeholder component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a placeholder lucide icon for tools so each tool row has a visual anchor
acceptanceCriteria: Renders a 16x16 Wrench icon from lucide-react as default placeholder
Accepts toolName: string (for future icon mapping), optional className
userStories.notes: import { Wrench } from 'lucide-react'; Simple wrapper. toolName for future mapping.

userStories.title: Redesign Header with RetroUI Badge
userStories.description: As a user, I want the header styled with RetroUI NeoBrutalism
acceptanceCriteria: Header uses border-b-2 border-border, shadow-[0_3px_0_0_var(--border)], bg-background
Connection status uses RetroUI Badge: connected = variant='surface' size='sm', disconnected = variant='outline' size='sm' with className for destructive color
userStories.notes: Import { Badge } from './retro/Badge'. Badge variants: surface = 'outline-2 bg-primary text-primary-foreground' (yellow connected), outline = 'outline-2 outline-foreground text-foreground' (disconnected, override with className='text-destructive outline-destructive').

userStories.title: Redesign Footer with RetroUI styling
userStories.description: As a user, I want the footer to match RetroUI NeoBrutalism
acceptanceCriteria: Footer uses border-t-2 border-border, bg-background, text-muted-foreground
userStories.notes: Current: border-t border-gray-800. Update to border-t-2 border-border bg-background text-muted-foreground.

userStories.title: Redesign PluginCard with RetroUI Accordion, PluginIcon, and Badges
userStories.description: As a user, I want each plugin displayed as a RetroUI Accordion card with a plugin icon and clear visual hierarchy
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard uses RetroUI Accordion for expand/collapse
AccordionItem classes from source: 'border-2 bg-background rounded text-foreground shadow-md hover:shadow-sm data-[state=open]:shadow-sm transition-all overflow-hidden'
Plugin header row: [PluginIcon 32x32] [name in font-head + badges row (version, tab state, trust)] [Switch far right]
PluginIcon at 32x32 with border-2 border-border bg-muted placeholder SVG
Tab state: Badge ready=variant='surface', unavailable=variant='default', closed=variant='outline'
Trust tier: Badge official=variant='solid', community=variant='surface', local=variant='outline'
Toggle-all uses RetroUI Switch (border-2 border-foreground, data-[state=checked]:bg-primary)
Switch click prevents accordion toggle via stopPropagation
Toggle errors shown using RetroUI Alert with status='error'
TabStateHint uses RetroUI theme colors (text-destructive for closed, text-muted-foreground for unavailable)
Optimistic toggle with rollback still works
userStories.notes: Import { Accordion } from './retro/Accordion', { Badge } from './retro/Badge', { Switch } from './retro/Switch', { Alert } from './retro/Alert', { PluginIcon } from './PluginIcon'. For the toggle error message, use Alert status='error': <Alert status='error' className='mx-3 mb-1 py-1 px-2 text-[11px]'>{toggleError}</Alert>. This replaces the old manual red bg div. For the accordion header with Switch: use AccordionItem and AccordionContent from RetroUI, but create a custom header using AccordionPrimitive.Header + AccordionPrimitive.Trigger directly from @radix-ui/react-accordion. This gives full control so the Switch sits outside the Trigger. The RetroUI AccordionHeader bakes a ChevronDown inside the Trigger which won't work with our layout. Keep a ChevronDown in the trigger area that rotates on open. accordion-down/up keyframes needed in styles.css: @keyframes accordion-down { from { height: 0 } to { height: var(--radix-accordion-content-height) } } @keyframes accordion-up { from { height: var(--radix-accordion-content-height) } to { height: 0 } }.

userStories.title: Redesign ToolRow with ToolIcon, RetroUI Switch, and Tooltip
userStories.description: As a user, I want each tool row to have a tool icon, RetroUI toggle, and tooltip
acceptanceCriteria: Each tool row starts with ToolIcon (16x16 Wrench placeholder) in text-muted-foreground
Tool name in font-medium text-foreground text-xs
Tool description truncated with RetroUI Tooltip on hover (variant='default': border-2 border-border bg-background)
RetroUI Switch for toggle (border-2 border-foreground, h-6 w-11, data-[state=checked]:bg-primary)
Active tool: spinner border-2 border-muted border-t-primary animate-spin + row border-l-2 border-primary
Rows separated by border-b border-border last:border-b-0
Layout: flex row [ToolIcon] [name+desc col] [spacer] [spinner if active] [Switch]
userStories.notes:
Import { Switch } from './retro/Switch', { Tooltip } from './retro/Tooltip', { ToolIcon } from './ToolIcon'. Tooltip pattern:
{description}
{description}.

userStories.title: Add search/filter input for tools
userStories.description: As a user, I want a search bar to filter tools by name or description
acceptanceCriteria: RetroUI Input rendered below header when more than 5 total tools
Input uses EXACT RetroUI Input classes: 'px-4 py-2 w-full rounded border-2 shadow-md transition focus:outline-hidden focus:shadow-xs'
Typing filters tools in real-time (case-insensitive on name + description)
Plugins with zero matching tools hidden entirely
Plugins with some matching tools show only matching tool rows
When filter active, each visible plugin shows '2 of 8 tools' count in text-muted-foreground
When zero matches across all plugins, show centered 'No tools matching ...' in text-muted-foreground
Filtering does NOT force-open collapsed cards
Clearing restores full unfiltered view with counts removed
Filter state in App.tsx, passed to PluginList
Clear button (X) when text present, Search icon as prefix
userStories.notes: Import { Input } from './retro/Input'. Wrap in relative div with Search icon absolute-left, X absolute-right. For filtered count in PluginCard: when toolFilter is active, show text-xs text-muted-foreground '2 of 8 tools'. For no-matches in PluginList: centered div text-sm text-muted-foreground py-8.

userStories.title: Redesign empty states using RetroUI Empty and Loader components
userStories.description: As a user, I want empty/loading states to use the actual RetroUI Empty and Loader components
acceptanceCriteria: DisconnectedState uses RetroUI Empty component (from source: 'flex flex-col items-center justify-center p-4 md:p-8 border-2 rounded shadow-md transition-all hover:shadow-none bg-card text-center')
DisconnectedState uses Empty.Icon with Unplug from lucide-react (replacing the old icon)
DisconnectedState uses Empty.Title with font-head for the heading
DisconnectedState uses Empty.Separator (a 'w-full h-1 bg-primary' divider)
DisconnectedState uses Empty.Description for the instruction text
The code block (bun --hot ...) is rendered inside Empty.Content with border-2 border-border bg-muted px-3 py-2
LoadingState uses RetroUI Loader component (bouncing dots with border-2 animate-bounce, default variant = primary-colored dots)
EmptyState (no plugins) uses RetroUI Empty component with Zap icon, title, separator, description
All states use RetroUI theme tokens (no hardcoded grays)
userStories.notes: Import { Empty } from './retro/Empty', { Loader } from './retro/Loader'. RetroUI Empty from source: compound component with Empty (container), Empty.Content, Empty.Icon (defaults to Ghost, but accepts children so pass or ), Empty.Title (uses Text as='h3'), Empty.Separator (w-full h-1 bg-primary), Empty.Description (text-muted-foreground max-w-[320px]). RetroUI Loader from source: bouncing dots. Default variant: '[&>div]:bg-primary [&>div]:border-border'. Renders 3 divs with border-2 animate-bounce and staggered delays. Use for loading state. DisconnectedState example: Not Connected Start the MCP server: bun --hot platform/mcp-server/dist/index.js

userStories.title: Redesign banners and ErrorBoundary using RetroUI Alert and Button
userStories.description: As a user, I want warning banners and error UI to use actual RetroUI Alert and Button components
acceptanceCriteria: VersionMismatchBanner uses RetroUI Alert with status='warning' (from source: 'bg-yellow-300 text-yellow-800 border-yellow-800')
VersionMismatchBanner uses Alert.Title for the warning heading
VersionMismatchBanner uses Alert.Description for the detail text
ErrorBoundary fallback uses RetroUI Alert with status='error' (from source: 'bg-red-300 text-red-800 border-red-800')
ErrorBoundary reload button uses RetroUI Button variant='default' size='sm'

Button has exact source classes: 'shadow-md hover:shadow active:shadow-none bg-primary text-primary-foreground border-2 border-black transition hover:translate-y-1 active:translate-y-2 active:translate-x-1 hover:bg-primary-hover'
userStories.notes:
Import { Alert } from './retro/Alert', { Button } from './retro/Button'. RetroUI Alert from source: base classes 'relative w-full rounded border-2 p-4'. Status variants: error='bg-red-300 text-red-800 border-red-800', warning='bg-yellow-300 text-yellow-800 border-yellow-800'. Alert.Title uses Text as='h5'. Alert.Description uses 'text-muted-foreground'. Button default uses 'border-black' not 'border-border' -- intentional in source. For VersionMismatchBanner:
<Alert status='warning'>
  <Alert.Title>Version Mismatch</Alert.Title>
  <Alert.Description>...</Alert.Description>
</Alert>
For ErrorBoundary:
<Alert status='error'>
  <Alert.Title>Something went wrong</Alert.Title>
  <Alert.Description>{error message}</Alert.Description>
  <Button variant='default' size='sm' onClick={reload}>Reload</Button>
</Alert>

userStories.title: Remove old ToggleSwitch, TrustBadge, and dead code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unused components removed and codebase clean
acceptanceCriteria: No remaining imports of ToggleSwitch or TrustBadge
All retro/ components imported by at least one consumer
PluginIcon and ToolIcon imported by their consumers
No unused imports or dead code
Old animate-tool-pulse removed from styles.css if no longer used
userStories.notes: Delete ToggleSwitch.tsx and TrustBadge.tsx. Run knip. Check if animate-tool-pulse is still referenced.
